Time-inconsistency: users might suddenly change their preferences in non-random ways

<div style="display: flex; gap: 1em;">
<div style="flex: 2; margin-bottom: -2em;">

::: {.callout-tip title="Definition: Hyperbolic Discounting"}
Hyperbolic discounting $\gamma(d) = \frac{1}{1 + kd}$ is used to model behaviors like procrastination in humans.
$$
Q(s,a \mid d) = \gamma(d) R(s,a) + \mathbb{E}_{s', a'}[Q(s', a' \mid d + 1)],
$$
where $s' \sim T(s, a), a' \sim \pi(\cdot \mid s)$.
:::

</div>
<div style="flex: 1; margin-top: -1.5em; margin-bottom: -2em;">

![](images/discounting.svg){width="100%"}

</div>
</div>

[@inconsistent]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}
